segment: anonymized founder team using cloud marketplace deployment
affected_workload: A tool-heavy agent reduces customer evidence from support, product, usage, CRM, and compliance sources before the model writes the final risk packet.
consequence: Teams on Bedrock or Vertex cannot use the programmatic tool calling path that keeps bulky custom-tool output out of model context, so the cost-saving pattern is blocked for marketplace deployments.
current_workaround: Use the direct Messages API where available, or keep tool-result reduction in app-owned code and pass only the compact packet back to Claude.
next_action: Close the availability gap, or document the fallback architecture for Bedrock and Vertex deployments.

learning_update.change: Add the deployment caveat and app-owned reducer fallback anywhere programmatic tool calling is taught.

founder_follow_up.note: Follow up after the fallback or availability answer is decided. Keep private notes outside public artifacts.

source_evidence.note: Shows the direct API token-saving receipt that makes the gap matter.
Records the Bedrock and Vertex availability qualifier.

replay.expected: The direct API path keeps raw custom-tool outputs out of model context and proves the cost lever.
replay.actual: The same pattern is not available to Bedrock or Vertex customers, so deployment path blocks production use.
